content:
It looks like a beginner exercise — a video on the left, a scrolling list of messages on the right, refresh it now and then. Most people sketch the component tree in two minutes and assume they’re done.
Then the interviewer asks the question the whole problem is actually about:
“A million people are watching. Thousands are typing every second. How do you show that on screen — in real time?”
And the honest answer is the twist: you don’t show it in real time, and you don’t show all of it. Getting to that answer — and defending it — is the entire interview.
Here’s the full reasoning, broken down.
🔴 Live demo — the finished widget is running here: YouTube Live Stream Chat on CodeSandbox. Open it in one tab and read along in another.
Step 1 — Clarify before you code
The biggest mistake is jumping straight to components. Surface the assumptions first. Questions worth asking:

• Where does the data come from? A real API, or a mock? Decides whether the conversation is about the…
